items.correct: Communist North Vietnam ultimately won, reuniting the country in 1975 after US forces had withdrawn, despite massive American intervention.
items.misconception: The USA won the Vietnam War.
items.random_wrong: China conquered Vietnam in the war.
items.why: Students assume US power guaranteed victory; superior firepower could not defeat a determined guerrilla and nationalist communist movement.

items.q: Was the communist world united during the Asian Cold War?
items.correct: China and the USSR increasingly clashed in the Sino-Soviet split, so communism in Asia was far from a single coordinated bloc.
items.misconception: All communist states acted as one united bloc directed from Moscow.
items.random_wrong: China and the USA were allies throughout the Cold War.
items.why: Learners assume monolithic communism; the Sino-Soviet split let Nixon later play China against the Soviet Union.

items.q: How did the Korean War end in 1953?
items.correct: It ended in an armistice with the border near the original 38th parallel, leaving Korea divided rather than unified.
items.misconception: The Korean War ended with a clear communist or American victory.
items.random_wrong: The Korean War ended with the conquest of China.
items.why: Students expect a decisive result; the war stalemated, and no formal peace treaty was ever signed, leaving Korea split.

items.q: Who led the communists to victory in China in 1949?
items.why: Correct answer: Mao Zedong. “Chiang Kai-shek” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How did the Korean War of 1950-53 end?
items.correct: In stalemate near the original border
items.misconception: With a communist conquest of all Korea
items.why: It's In stalemate near the original border. “With a communist conquest of all Korea” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What did the Domino Theory predict?
items.correct: Communism would spread country by country if unchecked
items.misconception: The USSR would invade the USA
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Communism would spread country by country if unchecked. “The USSR would invade the USA” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What was significant about Nixon's 1972 visit to China?
items.correct: It eased hostility between the USA and China
items.random_wrong: It united North and South Vietnam
items.why: It's It eased hostility between the USA and China. “It started the Korean War” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was the People's Republic of China established?
items.why: Exact answer: 1949 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
